commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4229215ce9c8dc364e8eb50a636fab57ab5e9ad3)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a shared ClawHub owner-match guard in the skill lifecycle code and regression tests for install, update, and verify owner-qualified mismatch behavior.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +156. Total +182 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source and contributor proof: current main can force install an owner-qualified skill into the same bare local slug before any owner-match check, and the PR body shows before/after runtime output. I did not run a live CLI reproduction in this read-only pass, so the review status is source-reproducible.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can update the docs for the new force-install contract; final merge still needs maintainer acceptance of the compatibility behavior.

target.title: fix(skills): refuse owner-qualified ClawHub force install over a different owner
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Installing an owner-qualified ClawHub skill with `--force` over a skill already installed under a different owner silently destroys the existing skill and rewrites its tracking to the new owner. With `@alice/weather` installed, `openclaw skills install @bob/weather --force` reports success while replacing Alice's skill with Bob's under the same local name and flipping the lockfile and origin `ownerHandle` to `bob`. There is no way to keep both and no warning. The CLI itself recommends `--force` when a plain install errors with "already exists", so this is an easy path to data loss of installed state.

Introduced by the owner-qualified install support in #94282 (ebd87e7f37).

## Why This Change Was Made

The owner-mismatch invariant (an owner-qualified action must not act on a skill tracked under a different owner) is one rule shared by every ClawHub lifecycle entry point, but only the update and verify paths enforced it; the install path did not. `performClawHubSkillInstall` derives `targetDir` and the lock/origin key from the bare `params.slug` with the owner dropped into `ownerHandle`, and under `--force` the `pathExists(targetDir)` short-circuit is skipped and `installPackageDir` does a backup-then-replace, so an `@bob/weather` install lands directly on top of the tracked `@alice/weather`.

This change routes all three surfaces (install, update, verify) through one strict `assertClawHubOwnerMatch` helper instead of three near-identical inline checks. The helper refuses whenever a requested owner differs from the tracked owner, including a tracked skill that has no owner.

The install path differs from update and verify in one principled way: on install the requested owner is the actual download source, while on update and verify it is only a guard assertion (both always re-fetch from the tracked source). So the install path consults the guard only when the tracked skill already has a known owner; an owner-less (legacy) tracked skill falls through to the existing ClawHub release and trust checks instead of being reported as an owner mismatch. Update and verify keep their pre-existing strict behavior unchanged, including refusing an owner-qualified action against an owner-less tracked skill.

## User Impact

- `install @owner/skill --force` over a skill tracked under a different owner now fails with `Skill "<slug>" is tracked as @<tracked>/<slug>, not @<requested>/<slug>.` and leaves the installed skill, lockfile, and origin untouched.
- Fresh installs, same-owner force reinstalls, owner-less force installs, and same-owner updates are unaffected.
- No config, schema, or migration changes.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`: 80 passed, including 5 new branch-complete regression cases (install different-owner refusal, install same-owner allowed, install owner-less fall-through, install owner-less with a blocked release, update owner-less refusal, verify owner-less refusal).
- oxlint on both changed files: clean.
- tsgo not run locally (OOM-constrained host); CI covers the type pass.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: owner-qualified `install --force` over a skill tracked under a different owner must refuse instead of silently downloading over it and rewriting its owner tracking, while every sibling owner-guard branch keeps its existing behavior.
Real environment tested: the real exported lifecycle runtime functions installSkillFromClawHub, updateSkillsFromClawHub, and resolveClawHubSkillVerificationTarget, driven on a real on-disk workspace seeded with origin.json plus lock.json, with only the ClawHub network boundary stubbed at the infra seam; run on pristine main@87db23e543 and on PR head 4229215ce9 with identical inputs.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: a standalone driver (since deleted) calls each function for six owner-guard branches and writes the real result values (ok, error, resulting lock ownerHandle, whether the release was fetched, whether the package install ran) to a file, executed once on pristine main and once on the patched head.
Evidence after fix:
```
# BEFORE (pristine main 87db23e543)
S1 install @bob/weather --force over tracked @alice/weather: ok=true error=- lock=bob@1.0.0 resolutionFetched=true installRan=true
S2 install @alice/weather --force over tracked @alice/weather: ok=true lock=alice@1.0.0 installRan=true
S3 install @bob/weather --force over owner-less tracked weather: ok=true lock=bob@1.0.0 resolutionFetched=true installRan=true
S4 install @bob/weather --force over owner-less tracked weather, release blocked: ok=false error="Release weather@1.0.0 is blocked by ClawHub security review." installRan=false
S5 update @demo-owner/weather over owner-less tracked weather: rejected: Skill "weather" is tracked as weather, not @demo-owner/weather. resolutionFetched=false
S6 verify @other-owner/weather over owner-less tracked weather: ok=false error="Skill \"weather\" is tracked as weather, not @other-owner/weather."
# AFTER (this patch, identical inputs, head 4229215ce9)
S1 install @bob/weather --force over tracked @alice/weather: ok=false error="Skill \"weather\" is tracked as @alice/weather, not @bob/weather." lock=alice@1.0.0 resolutionFetched=false installRan=false
S2 install @alice/weather --force over tracked @alice/weather: ok=true lock=alice@1.0.0 installRan=true
S3 install @bob/weather --force over owner-less tracked weather: ok=true lock=bob@1.0.0 resolutionFetched=true installRan=true
S4 install @bob/weather --force over owner-less tracked weather, release blocked: ok=false error="Release weather@1.0.0 is blocked by ClawHub security review." installRan=false
S5 update @demo-owner/weather over owner-less tracked weather: rejected: Skill "weather" is tracked as weather, not @demo-owner/weather. resolutionFetched=false
S6 verify @other-owner/weather over owner-less tracked weather: ok=false error="Skill \"weather\" is tracked as weather, not @other-owner/weather."
```
Observed result after fix: only S1 moves, from a silent clobber on main (ok=true, lock owner flipped to bob, release fetched, package install ran) to a clean refusal on the patched head (ok=false with the tracked-owner error, lock owner stays alice, nothing fetched, nothing installed); S2 same-owner install, S3 owner-less fall-through, and S4 owner-less blocked release stay allowed or blocked exactly as before, and S5 update plus S6 verify keep refusing the owner-less mismatch, so the new guard fixes the clobber without over-firing or regressing any sibling branch.
What was not tested: no live ClawHub HTTP request (the registry boundary was stubbed at the infra seam); no full build or type pass on this host (OOM-constrained); the thin CLI wrapper around these functions was not separately re-driven.
target.author: yetval

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
